senses.definition: a noisy, boisterous party or gathering, often with dancing and entertainment
examples: The wedding reception turned into a lively rumpuncheon that lasted until dawn.

senses.definition: a noisy commotion or disturbance
examples: There was a big rumpus outside the stadium after the game.
The children caused a rumpus in the hallway.

senses.definition: plural of rumpus; noisy commotions or disturbances
examples: The town woke to multiple rumpuses reported by neighbors.
They moved away after a series of rumpuses.

senses.definition: slightly dirty or smoky, or suggestive of sexual arousal; informal
examples: The old club had a rumpy, smoky air.
He gave a rumpy smile.

senses.definition: a person who illegally smuggles alcohol, especially during Prohibition
examples: The rumrunner was chased by coast guard ships.
Diaries describe many rumrunners at the border.

senses.definition: plural of rumrunner; people who illegally smuggle alcohol
examples: The film follows several rumrunners during the era.
Rumrunners faced severe penalties.

senses.definition: the act of illegally smuggling alcohol, especially during Prohibition
examples: Rumrunning became a lucrative business for some families.
Authorities cracked down on rumrunning routes.

senses.definition: plural of rum; a strong distilled alcoholic drink made from sugarcane byproducts
examples: He enjoyed two rums after dinner.
The bartender offered a selection of rums from the Caribbean.

senses.definition: a shop or bar that sells rum or other alcoholic drinks; a place for drinking
examples: We stopped at the rumshop for a quick refreshment.
The old town has several rumshops by the river.

senses.definition: a playful or informal term for a rum-based party or drink exchange; slang/nonce word
examples: They organized a rumswizzle to celebrate the weekend.
Bring a bottle for the rumswizzle.

senses.definition: a humorous or colloquial reference to a person or thing that is odd; sometimes used as a playful epithet
examples: That gadget is a real rumtytoo.
Don't mind the rumtytoo remarks; they’re in good fun.

senses.definition: to move quickly on foot; to operate or function; to run a program
examples: I run every morning to stay fit.
The car runs on gasoline.
She will run the meeting this afternoon.

senses.definition: a small, lightweight vehicle; something used for short trips
examples: They used a runabout to explore the coast.
The runabout was handy for quick errands.

senses.definition: plural of runabout; small vehicles or things used for short trips
examples: The shelves hold several runabouts for quick deliveries.
They collected runabouts from different docks.

senses.definition: archaic or obscure term; a traitor or turncoat in some dialects; may appear in historical texts
examples: The chronicle mentions a runagado who betrayed the town.
Scholars discuss the meaning of runagado in old folklore.

senses.definition: a person who abandons a cause or faction; a deserter
examples: The memoir denounces every runagate who fled the mission.
Runagate soldiers were shunned by their comrades.

senses.definition: plural of runagate; deserters or traitors
examples: The poem condemns runagates who betrayed the trust of the group.

senses.definition: a term from some Pacific languages or organizations; may refer to a council or assembly in indigenous contexts
examples: The runanga met to discuss community matters.
A runanga is respected for upholding traditional decisions.

senses.definition: plural of runanga; councils or assemblies in certain cultures
examples: Two runangas convened to discuss the treaty.
Runangas play a role in local governance.

senses.definition: a situation where someone is given many tasks or directions that waste time; a long, roundabout journey
examples: I got the runaround from the customer service desk.
We had to go through a runaround to get the permit.

senses.definition: plural of runaround; series of delays or detours
examples: The runarounds they faced were frustrating.
We endured several runarounds before approval.

senses.definition: a person who has left home or a place without permission; a horse or vehicle that can run away
examples: The runaway left town at midnight.
The runaway horse caused chaos in the square.

senses.definition: happening or moving away from a place; out of control
examples: The runaway train was speeding toward the station.
Prices have been on a runaway rise.

senses.definition: plural of runaway; people who have left home without permission; runaway animals or vehicles
examples: Several runaways were reported by the shelter.
The runaways were eventually found safe.

senses.definition: a return leg or reversal of a route or action; in sports or logistics
examples: They planned a runback to complete the circuit.
The runback in the supply chain reduced downtime.

senses.definition: plural of runback; return trips or replays
examples: They completed two runbacks before the deadline.
